CONTENT.Honours: Manchester CityFA Cup: 2022–23UEFA Champions League: 2022–23UEFA Super Cup: 2023
CONTENT.Club career: Perrone made his professional debut for Vélez Sarsfield on 6 March 2022 in match against Estudiantes. On 18 May 2022 he scored his first professional goal in Copa Libertadores match against Nacional.On 23 January 2023, Perrone signed a five-and-a-half-year contract with Manchester City for around £8m, and joined the club following the conclusion of the 2023 South American U-20 Championship. On 25 February, he made his Premier League debut in a 4–1 away win over Bournemouth, replacing Erling Haaland in the 72nd minute.On 23 August 2023, Perrone joined La Liga side Las Palmas on loan for the entirety of the 2023–24 season.
CONTENT.External links: Profile at the Manchester City F.C. websiteMáximo Perrone – UEFA competition record (archive)Máximo Perrone at Soccerway
CONTENT.International career: Perrone represented Argentina at under-16 and under-20 levels.In March 2023, he received his first call-up to the Argentina senior national team by head coach Lionel Scaloni for two friendly matches against Panama and Curaçao.

SUMMARY: Máximo Perrone (born 7 January 2003) is an Argentine professional footballer who plays as a defensive midfielder for Premier League club Manchester City.
